description_long: This lantern features a robust, hexagonal base crafted from a dark, marble-like material with white veining, giving it a heavy and durable appearance. The upper section is adorned with translucent yellow panels, possibly glass, set into the sides, which likely allow light to diffuse outward. The top of the lantern is open, revealing an orange, textured surface that suggests a glowing or illuminated core, possibly simulating a fire or light source. The geometric, faceted design and the use of contrasting colors—black, yellow, and orange—lend the lantern a modern yet ornamental look. The structure appears solid and stable, suitable for placement on flat surfaces in urban environments such as parks, plazas, or sidewalks. Decorative elements are subtle, focusing on the interplay of materials and the faceted, angular form.
description: A hexagonal, marble-like lantern with yellow glass panels and an orange illuminated core, featuring a sturdy, ornamental design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the hexagonal shape, yellow glass panels, and the orange core clearly visible at the top.
description_view_1: This angled view highlights the faceted base and the way the yellow glass panels wrap around the lantern.
description_view_2: Similar to the front, this view emphasizes the symmetry of the hexagonal design and the glowing orange core.
description_view_3: This side angle reveals the depth of the yellow glass panels and the textured marble base.
